1 | June 2013
For over five years now we’ve been publishing the
DotComSecrets” journal. Tens of thousands of people around
the world have been subscribers and watched their businesses
grow each month because of the things we’ve shared. It has
been the longest running and most successful print newsletter
in our industry.
But recently we decided that we wanted to make a change.
Partially because our business has changed so much over the last
five years, and partially because we’ve seen that the thing most
people ask us about, and the thing that has the biggest impact on
our subscribers’ businesses is when we share our testing results
That way you can see behind the scenes what we’re testing,
what’s working and then you can implement those tests back into
your own businesses. So, that is the purpose of the new format of
June 2013
ISSUE NO. 1
FROM RUSSELL

Matter

The New Marketing Secret
Weapon

Two Success Stories in Solo
Advertising and
Social Media Marketing
DCS-X

Goldmineor Waste of
Time?
fromRUSSELL

-- 1 of 40 --

2 | June 2013
this newsletter, now renamed “DotComSecrets Labs.” Our goal with DotComSecrets Labs
is to help you grow your business fast. As we’ve found, there are only really three ways to
grow a business:
• 	First, increase the number of visitors (traffic)
• 	Second, increase the number of customers (conversion)
• 	Third, increase how often they buy (backend)
So, we’ll be splitting up our newsletter each month and sharing tests in these three areas:
• 	Traffic tests (sources, ads, etc.)
• 	Conversion tests (on page tests)
• 	Backend tests (closing, webinars, sales processes, etc.)
Each month we will also do a LIVE webinar discussing the tests and showing you behind
the scenes how we’re determining what to test, how we pick winners and more! Make sure
you login to the members area at www.DotComSecretsLabs.com to see the back archives
and watch the members only webinars!
Now, while we are constantly doing testing in our businesses (and because we’re in 12
different niches / industries and we’re able to see a lot of things across multiple channels)
– we are ALWAYS looking to publish tests that you are running as well. So as you find tests
that are working in your business, please submit them to us and you may see your name
and tests in print! Thanks again to everyone who’s made this publication possible. I hope
everyone loves the new format and that our tests will help you in your business!
Russell Brunson
The “Overnight Success Maker”

are based upon the current internet marketing environment. The
techniques presented have been extraordinarily lucrative and
rewarding. Because the internet is constantly changing, the sites
and services presented in this book may change, cease or expand
with time. We hope that the skills and knowledge acquired from
this course will provide you with the ability to adapt to inevitable
internet evolution. However, we cannot be held responsible for
changes that may affect the applicability of these techniques.
Any earnings, income statements or other results, are
based on our own testing and are only estimates of what we
think you could earn. There is no assurance you will do as
well as stated in any examples. If you rely upon any figures
provided, you must accept the entire risk of not doing as well
as the information provided.

archives. All product names, logos and artwork are copyrights

or organizations are unintentional. The purchaser or reader
of this publication assumes responsibility for the use of these
materials and information. No guarantees of income are made.

responsibility or liability whatsoever on behalf of any purchaser
or reader of these materials.

DotComSecrets Labs | 3
By Russell Brunson, the Overnight Success Maker
Last night at about 11:30 at night, I got a text from
one of my friends. All it had was a link to an article on
TechCrunch.com. I could tell from the title of the article
it had something to do with a project that I was involved
in. The title of the article was: “What Not To Do In Your
Startup Promo Video” and under that headline I saw
my
video that I made for the Rippln launch.
My hands started to sweat, butterflies gathered in
my stomach. I inhaled, held my breath and started to
scroll down and read as the writer tore my video apart
sentence-by-sentence, second-by-second showing
why
he thought it was horrible. After about the third
paragraph, I slowly let out my breath, my tense muscles
loosened and a smile started to come across my face.
Everything in his article was based on his opinion. And
while I respect the right for anyone to have an opinion, I
know from my own experience that my opinion is often
wrong (especially when it comes to marketing).
Now, what this author may or may not have known was
that in the seven days prior to him posting his review about
my video, we had released it to the world. In the video I said
it would go viral... it went viral. In the video I said the news
would cover it... and they have... In the video I told people
that their friends and even strangers would approach
them about it... and they have. I was literally prophetic on
everything that was stated in that video.
And not only has it started to grow, in a little over a week
I inhaled, held my breath and started to scroll down
and read as the writer tore my video apart second-by-
second, showing why
he thought it was horrible...”
fromRUSSELL
YOUR
OPINION
DOESN’T 	MATTER
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 40 --

4 | June 2013
that video has helped to get over 220,000 people to join our
little movement and we’re adding over 30,000 people
a day!
Yes, our initial growth is surpassing the initial growth
of Facebook, Twitter and pretty much any and all other
social networks that have ever come out. Will that
growth sustain? I have no idea, but one thing I do know
is that
this video (ya, the one he said was the worst
startup video ever) has already proven to be the exact
opposite of that statement.
What I’ve learned since we started our business, is that our
opinions matter very little. In the hundreds of tests we run
every month, often times my opinion on the better looking
page loses. And when that happens, I’ve got two options.
I can stick to my opinion and keep running with it despite
the fact that I was wrong. Or the second, (and a much
smarter and more profitable idea) is to swallow my pride
and realize that my opinion doesn’t matter. The
only thing
that matters when you are trying to build a business and
make money, is what works. Results speak for themselves.
When you have a page design that you
hate, but it makes you
300 times more money... swallow your pride. When you have a
video that annoys you, but it gets 200,000 new members in 10
days... swallow your pride. And if you’re really smart, and you
see someone else doing something awesome that’s working,
instead of making fun of it, model it. DCS
Scan the QR code or go to
the website below to view
the video and post:
http://techcrunch.com/2013/04/22/
what-not-to-do-in-your-startup-
promo-video/
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 40 --

6 | June 2013
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
We were going to promote our “Free Website” squeeze
page to one of the lists at Arcamax, and decided to test four
different headlines:
• 	Congrats your Free Website is Ready!
• 	Get 5 checks each month (free website)
• 	Just checking... Did you get your free website?
• 	Your *FREE* Website Valued At $2,079!
As you can see from the test results above, one headline
was by far the winner... But what was really cool were a
few additional things we did with that headline after we
discovered it was the winner. We took that headline and made
it the title of the clickable links which increased clickthrough
rates on the actual email by over 30%.
Our thoughts are because the subject line was good enough
that it caused them to open the email... this
same subject would
cause them to click more on the links inside of the email. So, if
you want to make 30% more money from every email you send
out, match the link text and the winning subject line text.
Our next test is to change the headline on the landing page
to match the one used in the subject line. My guess is that
because it keeps the congruency from the sales process that it
will also increase conversions. I’ll report back in a future issue
as soon as we have stats from that test.
Winning Solo Ad Subject Line1
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 6 of 40 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 7
Test by Justin and Chaunna Brooke
A note from Russell: Justin and Chaunna Brooke from IMScalable.com sent us
some very interesting tests (traffic tests 2 - 6) with some of the ads that they
are running. This first test forced me to get about 60 new banners designed:
We tested the black vs. white banner because we noticed while looking at
classified ads in the back of a magazine that the darker ads caught our eyes.
Sure enough when we tested it the black banner pulled ahead of our winner.
We believe it was because it stood out on the page and also did not look like an
advertisement. The plain banner had a CTR of .34% and the black one did .48%.
Test by Justin and Chaunna Brooke
B was the winner and got twice the CTR and
cut costs in half. In case you cannot see the
image, A had a .038% CTR and B had .079%.
The cost per click on A was $0.81 and for B
it was $0.40. The reason why is because B
tells a story and creates more curiosity. It also
promises a fast and easy solution with a call
to action to “see proof” that the claim is legit.
Black vs. White2
Click Through Rate
Ad A (White Background) 	Ad B (Black Background)
.34% 	.48%
Ad “A” With
White Background Ad “B” With
Black Background
Black vs. White3
41%INCREASE
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 7 of 40 --

8 | June 2013
Test by Justin and Chaunna Brooke
The only difference in these ads was that one of them took the visitor off of
Facebook and the other one took them to a fan page tab.
The fan page tab had the same exact page as the other ad, just wrapped into an
iframe. The ad that took them off of Facebook had a .072% CTR and was costing
$0.44 per click. Meanwhile the same ad taking them to a fan page tab cost only
$0.17 per click and had a blistering CTR of 0.792%.
Test by Justin and Chaunna Brooke
Ad A was getting .031% CTR and ROI was at NEGATIVE 60% because it was
trying to rely on curiosity alone. B got .068% CTR and brought in a 200% ROI
because it was specific, offered curiosity, and stated a benefit.
C blew our minds with a .945% CTR but caused a massive drop in conversions. We
think the reason for this is that people just wanted to see the picture explained or
larger. It also did not preframe the audience for the “green coffee bean” like B did.
On or Off Facebook?4

Cost Per Click

Sending Traffic Off
Facebook Sending Traffic to a
Facebook Fanpage

-- 8 of 40 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 9
Test by Justin and Chaunna Brooke
Fancy banners have lost in our split tests almost every single time. The only time
they won was for luxury items and design related offers (obvious).
If you want
really good banner ads, make them
not look like banner ads. The
winner here looks like a featured article when it’s on other people’s sites. Which
turns off the viewers natural resistance to advertising.
The fancy banner had a CTR of .11% and the plain banner had a CTR of .34%.
Fancy Vs. Basic Design6Ad A (Fancy Design) 	Ad B (Basic Design)
Click Through Rate
.11% .34%Ad “A” 	Ad “B”
Event Date On Banner Ad7
Test by Mike Cooch
QUESTION: When advertising for an event. Will having the
date in the ad result in a higher CTR?
HYPOTHESIS/ASSUMPTION: This test hopes to prove that the
use of an event’s date in a Google Adwords remarketing ad
will produce a higher CTR than one without.
Experiment:
AUDIENCE: Both rounds of this experiment used the same
audience. The following remarketing lists were used:
• 	All Site Visitors - LIL
• 	All Visitors To Local Income Lab
These lists include any visitors that have landed on any
localincomelab.com page including the current date and up
to 30 days prior.
BUDGET: Both rounds were given a $50/day budget with a
maximum bid of $4. This budget has proven in the past to
209%INCREASE
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 9 of 40 --

10 | June 2013
Round 1 (With Event Date) 	Round 2 (Without Date)
resulting in a .32% CTR. The average CPC was $2.62, making
our total spend $220.14. Round 2 had a total of 44,370
impressions and 107 clicks resulting in a .24% CTR. The
average CPC was $1.71 giving a total spend of $107 for round
2. The average visit duration for round 1 was three times
longer than that of round 2.
FINDINGS: These test results indicate that the use of the date
in the ad resulted in a better click through rate. Round 1 had
about half the amount of impressions but still produced a
marginally higher CTR than round 2. This is significant
enough to note that it is important to use the date in an ad
when promoting an event.
What This Means For Your Business:
If you are hosting or promoting an event, advertising using
Google Adwords for remarketing is a good tool to use.
You will be able to reach a selected audience that is not
completely cold traffic. You will want to be sure to include
the date of your event in the ad. This will provide you with a
visitor who understands the absolute basics of your event.
From our data, you will find that the visitors will spend
more time on your page by using the date in the ad. This
could be attributed to having more qualified visitors going
to the page. They are not guessing when the event is and
immediately leaving the page when they see the date. These
visitors are aware of the when and focusing more on the
details of the event rather than their calendar.
provide enough data to analyse for a test such as this.
ROUND 1: In this round we will be using ads that have the
event’s date. The ad sizes used were 1 300x250, 1 728x90,
and 1 160x600.
ROUND 2: In this round we will be using ads that do not have
the event’s date. The ad sizes used were 1 300x250, 1 728x90,
and 1 160x600.
TEST RESULTS: Round 1 had 26,222 impressions 84 clicks
Conversion Rate:
.32% 	.24%
Round 1:
With Date Round 2:
Without Date
Cost Per
Click
$2.62Round 1: With Date
$1.71Round 2: Without Date

12 | June 2013
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
Todd had an idea that the long form sales letter was way too long for a free
trial. So against all logic he copied the “Start Your Free Trial” button that we
only showed after we revealed the price, and put it at the top of the page. The
results? A lot more money in my pocket.
It seems this month that on a few of our products with trials it’s been easier to
give people the ability to buy a lot earlier. Maybe we’ve been overselling our
customers (and maybe you are too...).
Big Annoying Button Raised
Conversions by 25%!
1
Control 	No Free Trial Button At Top
2% 	1.5%
Conversion Rate:
Control 	No Free Trial
Button At Top
26%INCREASE
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 12 of 40 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 13
Conversion Rate: Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
This test should not surprise me, but it always does. The designer in me always
wants my headlines to match my site design, but the marketer knows that red
almost always wins. Well, this split test showed exactly the same result. Blue
looked better, but red made me three times more money.
Now, there was one other interesting test that we almost didn’t run, but
noticed another site with a similar product had navigation buttons on it.
Normally adding more places for people to click off of our site always hurts,
but in this test removing the navigation buttons below the add to cart button
decreased sales. I guess our customers needed a little more information before
they were willing to purchase.
3% 12.5%Control 	Red Headline
I’m Seeing Red2Control 	Red Headline
313%INCREASE
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 13 of 40 --

14 | June 2013
Don’t Scare “Cold” Traffic3
9% 	10%
Conversion Rate:
Control 	Remove 3-Step
Header
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
As you know, conversions and tests don’t always stay consistent across all
traffic sources. One interesting thing we found is that with “warm” traffic (from
my own lists of people I know) having the three steps above the form always
wins on conversions (as shown on Page A) but with cold traffic (banner ads,
ppc, etc...) it seems to scare them away as demonstrated on this test.
It seems that the three-step header intimidates cold traffic as they want one
easy step to get their result and showing three steps just discourages them
from starting the process. The moral of this story? Test each traffic source
independently from the others.
Here are some other interesting things we learned from this set of tests... First,
the red headline wins again! Yes, the red headline still dominates in every test
we’ve done recently - even when it makes the page ugly.
Second, social media buttons were not a big enough loser to recommend
removing for most people. They did make a difference but the expected gain
from traffic made up for it on ours. But the big thing we noticed is the 'credibility
gain' from social media icon numbers is pretty much BS since optins decreased...
It's possible with higher numbers on the social icons this might change...
Increase In Profits:
2.4%over control when
social buttons were
removed
51%over control
with red headline
Page A (Control) 	Remove 3-Step Header
10%INCREASE
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

DotComSecrets Labs | 15
Conversion Rate:
19% 	21%Expensive Design 	Ugly Blue Version
Expensive Design 	Ugly Blue Version
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
This test makes me laugh because we ran it for a week and we knew which one
was the definite winner. At about that time, one of our big JV partners asked if
they could promote our webinar, but stated that they didn’t want to promote to
the “ugly blue version.”
Yes, this version is ugly, but for whatever reason, it wins in almost every test
we’ve ever run. It even beats out pages that I’ve spent thousands of dollars on
awesome videos.
And then on the blue version when we tested long copy against short copy,
we found that the shorter copy increased optins by 26%. Our guess is that on
this long/short issue is we didn’t need the hard sell for the free webinar. Less
distractions equals more optins.
We found that the
shorter copy increased
optins by 26 percent.”
The Ugly Blue Version4
11%INCREASE
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 15 of 40 --

16 | June 2013
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
One of my close friends Daegan Smith has been telling me for over a year that
his new squeeze page with “micro-commitments” will out convert anything else
I can come up with. So I thought I’d put it to the test.
The concept behind how this style of squeeze page works, is instead of asking
for a big commitment up front (their email), start by asking them a simple
question (in this case, “how did you hear about us”) and then after they’ve
made that first micro-commitment to answer your question, then you ask
them for their email address.
The first screenshot shows our control and the second shows the same
squeeze page using Daegan’s layout. As you can see, Daegan’s style crushed
our control. It had double conversion on the optins but more importantly
it had almost all of the sales. We are going to start testing these micro-
commitments in various other spots in our sales funnels.
Micro-Commitment Squeeze Pages5Control 	Micro-Commitment Style
18% 35%
Conversion Rate:
Control 	Micro
Commitment
100%chance to beat original
95%INCREASE
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 16 of 40 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 17
Sell Me Something Already6
Control 	No Order Form Delay
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
The results of this test went against everything we had ever heard and assumed
was right. For years we had assumed as truth that on a sales video you should not
show the order form until the price was disclosed. Seems logical, right?
Well, on this test, it was completely wrong. Was it our audience? Was it our
traffic? Was it our offer? I’m not sure, but on these tests showing the order
form immediately when they showed up under the sales video increased our
revenues by 91.65%!
So, I’m not saying that this will win in all situations, but I would say you need
to test this on any of your funnels ASAP... you may just give yourself a 91% pay
increase overnight like we did.
Increase In Profits:
92%more money earned
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 17 of 40 --

18 | June 2013
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
Typically we put trust guard seals on almost all of our pages, and typically they
increase our conversions across the board. But on our automated webinar sales
pages, for some reason it hurt conversions by 13%.
Maybe it caused too much distraction to get the optin. This is just another
good example showing why you should always be testing.
15% 	13%
Conversion Rate:
Control 	With Trust
Guard Logos
Too Much Trust7
Control 	With Trust Guard Logos
To Facebook Or Not To Facebook?8 Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
We were relaunching our DotComSecrets-X sales funnel and had a few core
tests we wanted to try with the new surge of traffic we were sending through
this funnel. We setup four versions of the landing page:
• 	The first was just our normal page (had testimonials copied and pasted
from Facebook).
• 	The second page was the same, but instead of making the testimonials look
nice, we took actual screenshots of their comments under the video.
• 	And the third test was to have the “Add To Cart” button on the right hand
$.43revenue/visitor
with FB style
testimonials. Up
$.32 over control.
Increase In Profits:
296%with Facebook style
testimonials
13%INCREASE
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 18 of 40 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 19
side of the video instead of under it.
What we found was very interesting... First, the CTA
on the side of the video completely bombed... (which
makes me sad because we did two launches where we
had the CTA on the right side of the video... makes me
wonder how much money I lost because of that...)
Second, we found out that people put a lot of weight
on testimonials from Facebook. My guess is that people
believe that if it was posted on Facebook, that it’s a true
opinion. Since this test, we are replacing all testimonials
with Facebook style testimonials (even those not
collected on Facebook and we are formatting so they
look like they came from Facebook), and so far we are
seeing a huge lift across the board.
Control 	+ FB Testimonials 	Right Side Add to Cart
Winning testimonial style that
resulted in 296% improvement
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 19 of 40 --

20 | June 2013
Test by Roland Frasier
Over the last year we’ve been placing a small affiliate tab in the
upper right corner of our video sales letters in hopes of bolstering
our affiliate program. The thought here was that although it
might suppress conversions slightly, the pros of gaining new
affiliates would dramatically outweigh the cons long term.
This is one of those tests that goes against common sense. A
large obtrusive affiliate tab on the sales letter didn’t suppress
conversions at all. The sample size of over 35,000 unique
visitors and 750 total conversions and these versions resulted
in an absolute tie.
Here’s the kicker... during this test, version A created 37 more
new affiliates then version B (on both versions there is a small
affiliates link in the footer).
Most businesses are looking for any marketing channel they
can use to increase their profits. Affiliate programs are a
popular, yet underutilized channel particularly because it’s
hard to build an affiliate base. If you go to most websites
9 Affiliate Tab On Sales Letter
With Affiliate Tab 	Without Affiliate Tab
Conversion Rate:
2.1% 	2.1%
With Affiliate Tab 	Without
Affiliate Tab
37more affiliates signed up
with the tab than without.
you will likely see an affiliate link hidden away in small text
somewhere in the footer. This test shows that you don’t
have to sacrifice immediate sales to bolster your affiliate
base. Furthermore, you will be putting more people into
your affiliate program that in turn, will drive more sales in the
future. If implemented, this could be a real game changer for
your company. As always, I recommend that you test this for
yourself before implementing.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 20 of 40 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 21
10
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
This test was one of those that I thought that I
knew which
was going to be the winner, and I was completely wrong.
I thought that it would be important to show everyone
what they were receiving, and then strengthen it with
testimonials.
Todd decided to throw in a test where we switched the
order and had the testimonials first, and then show what
they received last.
After a very short battle, Todd’s version destroyed mine in
conversions as you can see here:
But more importantly in revenues as you can see here:
It’s actually very interesting because oftentimes in my head
I think that it would be better to tell a story and get people
engaged before showing any testimonials (which reveal that
there is a product), but in this case (and one very interesting
test that I’m going to show you next month), it has been
better to lead with testimonials. The next big test that I am
excited to try on our order forms is switching the “form”
from the left hand side to the right hand side.
Facebook Testimonials On Top
Control 	Testimonials On Top
Conversion Rate:
18%
35%Control 	Testimonials
On Top
96%INCREASE
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 21 of 40 --

22 | June 2013
11 Building Your Hot List
Test by Mike Filsaime, Written by Russell Brunson
I’ve always gone back and forth on if we should add a new
step after prospects are ready to buy, but before you take
someone to the order from. The powerful thing about this
strategy is you can increase conversions by following up
with people who go to the order form, but never actually
purchase. But my concern has always been that if we do this,
would the percentage of people we lose who would have
gone to the order form decrease because they have to optin
to get to the order form?
This test run by Mike Filsaime makes me feel a lot more
comfortable about adding in the extra “hot list” step before
the order page. The sales (pre-followup) seemed to stay
about the same at around 30%, but the optin percentage is
an amazing 80.39% giving us more than 80% of the people
we can follow up with to increase our sales percentage.
Now, the other cool part of this test is where they sent people
in the follow up series. They tried an order form that had
the calculator, one without the calculator and one with a
countdown clock that you can see above. As you can see
adding the sense of urgency with the countdown clock
proved to be the winner.
Hot List Step 	Countdown Clock (follow up)
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 22 of 40 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 23
Tested by Mike Filsaime, Written by Russell Brunson
This was a cool test that would work well in any type of
business where people could calculate their results. So
anything make money related, diet, finance, etc...
Under the sales video they added a calculator where
someone could calculate how much money they would
make with the system when they used it. As you can
see, adding the calculator did help increase the number
of people that went to the order form and increased
conversions.
The Calculator Close12
Conversion Rate:
12% 	11%With Calculator 	No Calculator
Video Spoiler Box13
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
This test was an idea that Mike Filsaime gave to us. Under his video sales letter
he added a spoiler box that had a quick description on what would be taught in
the video they were watching. This block sits under the sales video and shows
them what to expect in the video they are currently watching.
Since then we have added these boxes to all of our sales videos and so far they have
won on every test. I think the key to a good video spoiler box is making sure that the
things you show are engaging and cause the person to want to watch the rest of
the video. This is a simple addition to any video that will shoot up conversions fast!
16% 4%
Conversion Rate:
Control 	Spoiler Box
Hidden
8%INCREASE
73%INCREASE
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 23 of 40 --

24 | June 2013
Size of Sales Video14
Test by Mark Ling
We split tested the size of the video. We
didn’t think it would make a difference, but it
proved to make quite a noticeable one.
We tested 720 x 400 vs 640 x 360. We
later tested 720 wide vs 800 wide on
three occasions and noticed negligible
improvement (800 won by about five sales
across about 200 sales).
720 x 400 Size Video
Video Spoiler Box
Conversion Rate:
720 x 400 	640 x 360
3.6% 2.5%
31%INCREASE
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 24 of 40 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 25
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 25 of 40 --

26 | June 2013
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd
Dickerson
Last summer I was sitting on a house
boat with Kirt Christiansen who has an
automated webinar right now in the trading
niche that is killing it. While we were sitting
on the boat, he pulled his phone out of his
pocket and showed me his email inbox.
What I saw shocked me. It was two pages of
emails from people who had called him and
left their credit cards in a voice mail! I asked
what he did, and he told me that in his
webinar, when it reaches his CTA, he gives
his customers the ability to order online, or
they can call and place the order... but he
did it with a twist.
If they wanted to buy, but they had a question
before they ordered, he had them call a
Google voice number, and leave their full
name, address, phone, email and credit card
number (with expiration). He would then call
back the next day, answer their question and
then bill their credit card. But the key to this
was to stress that they needed to leave their
credit card to lock in their position.
I got excited by that, so when I got back
home I found his webinar, watched it and
saw what he did. I then went back into my
own automated webinar that had been
running for months, and tweaked one slide
(shown here).
Immediately we started getting phone calls
each day. After the dust settled, we looked
at the stats and saw an immediate increase
in profits by over 25 percent.
My thoughts are there is a percentage of
your customers who want to buy, but they
have just one question keeping them from
calling. If you give them the ability to get
that one question answered, they will buy.
The Slide That Immediately Gave
Us A 25% Raise!
1
Increase
In Profits:
25%by adding the
option to call
There is a percentage of
your customers who want
to buy, but they have just
one question keeping
them from calling...”
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 26 of 40 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 27
PayPal’s “Bill Me Later” Financing Puts
35% More Money Into Our Pocket!
2
PayPal Bill Me Later Option 	StoreCoach.com Using BML
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
I want to preface this test with the fact that if I trusted
PayPal, I’d use this on everything that I sold. In every webinar
pitch I did, I’d hard pitch the fact that prospects can get
everything financed by PayPal. But I’ve had too many PayPal
accounts shut down, so we were a little timid with this test,
but still were able to get a huge 30 to 35 percent increase in
sales on one of our high ticket offers. Let me explain.
PayPal’s “Bill Me Later” program lets your customers buy
your product, you get the money immediately, but your
customers don’t have to pay any interest (or anything)
for six months... But here’s the kicker – after 60 days, they
can’t refund, and PayPal does all of the collections for you!
Pretty cool, don’t you think?
So, if I was pitching this hard on a webinar, my script
would be, “and to prove that what I have works, I’m
gonna let you buy it, and use it for six full months before
you ever have to pay a dime... that way you can make
your money back before you pay anything...” I’m sure that
if I used that pitch I could have easily gotten over a 50%
increase, but alas, I’m scared of PayPal stealing my money.
So, what we did instead, after people left the order flow, we
live chat exit popped them about a financing option... (which
was just us pushing to a version of our order form with a
video at the top showing them how to use “Bill Me Later.”
We also added one email to the follow up sequence for
webinar attendees who viewed but did not purchase.
Between these two things we saw an immediate increase in
sales by 30% to 35% on a $725 offer.
After mentioning this to a few friends, I saw our buddies at
StoreCoach.com implement it as one of their core buying
options (even given higher priority than their normal order
button). I don’t know their conversion rate, but I’m sure this
gave them a nice lift.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 27 of 40 --

28 | June 2013
Test by Mike Cooch
One of my friends, Mike Cooch from LocalIncomeLabs.com,
sent us a really cool test that I wanted to show you because
the only real “tweak” was the actual offer. Sometimes we
focus so much on the little tests, that we forget the biggest
thing we can change (that will often have the biggest
impact) is our offer. Check out the huge increase Mike saw
by tweaking his offer:
We just re-launched Smarter Webinar, a $47/month
automated webinar service to our list. We took two
approaches and did a little split test:
Promo #1
Sell a valuable, low end offer ($19 for 20 done for you
webinar presentations, email scripts, etc.), then sell Smarter
Webinar as the upsell with the offer to discount the first
month. So the total they paid today was only $47, the
normal cost of one month of Smarter Webinar.
The “Dan Kennedy” Offer3 Dan Kennedy Style Offer
Promo #2
Sell Smarter Webinar directly, but offer the same 20 webinar
presentations as an "amazing free gift" just for trying the
service" (Dan Kennedy style). Here are the results:
Promo #1 Results
• 	$6.25 EPC
• 	Recurring Smarter Webinar accounts sold: 34
• 	Total Revenue: $3,441
• 	Total Recurring Revenue: $1,598
Promo #2 Results
• 	$8.64 EPC
• 	Recurring Smarter Webinar accounts sold: 153
• 	Total Revenue: $7,191
• 	Total Recurring Revenue: $7,191
I guess we know the right way to promote continuity to our
list! Even if we see much more churn from the Promo #2
buyers, we are going to come out way ahead.
Low End Offer With Upsell
Dan Kennedy Style Offer
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 28 of 40 --

www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 29 of 40 --

30 | June 2013
By Brett Curry, CEO of OnlineMarketingGiant.com
The hype surrounding online video right now is huge, and
this is one case where the hype is true. Consider some of
these staggering figures. One hour of video is uploaded
to YouTube every second. YouTube attracts more than
four billion views per day. More people conduct searches
on YouTube each day than do on Bing or Yahoo. And
YouTube is just one video sharing site out of hundreds
(though it is by far the biggest).
Considering these facts, do you think you might be able
to use video to build your business? No question about it.
Video is a proven winner. It’s a winner at driving traffic, and
for increasing conversions. Sharing and optimizing a video
on YouTube can send a steady stream of traffic to your sites.
Video on your site can help turn visitors into customers
by driving opt-ins, phone calls, and purchases. Video is
also a great tool for building deeper relationships with
current customers prompting them to order more and refer
more. The cool part is that it’s never been easier to create
online videos than it is right now. Handheld HD cameras
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 30 of 40 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 31
are inexpensive and most smart phones give the ability
to shoot HD anywhere you go. My favorite new video
creation tool is Google Hangouts.
Google Hangouts are a feature of Google+ and they are just
plain awesome. Simply put, Google Hangouts allow for free
video conferencing with up to 10 people. Choose an on-air
or public hangout and an unlimited number of people
can view your hangout while it occurs. You can share your
screen, or get on camera with your webcam. You can also
spice things up with sound effects and other cool Google
effects (my favorite is the animated pirate hat).
The coolest part about Hangouts is that you can quickly
and easily create YouTube videos of your hangouts.
Simply choose an “On Air” hangout when you launch
it and once you end the hangout the recording will
automatically be converted into a YouTube video. No
editing, no uploading, just an almost instant YouTube
video. That YouTube video can then be optimized, shared,
or embedded on your site. Here are a few creative video
ideas you can execute with Google Hangouts:
1. Interview A Satisfied Customer
Find a satisfied customer who isn’t camera shy and invite
them onto a hangout. Keep it simple and conversational
and ask your customer about their experience with your
business. It’s fast, easy and done properly will create a
powerful form of social proof.
2. Do A Product Demo
Want to show how your product works? Get in front of
TIPS FOR YOUR
NEXT 	HANGOUT
Add Guests and Panelists to
Your Circles First
Those you invite on the hangout with you
should be in your circles and you as the host
should be in their circles.
Choose the “On Air” Option
If you don’t choose the on-air option,
your Hangout will not be converted into a
YouTube video.
Add Google Effects for Fun
With Google effects you can add sound
effects like a cash register ringing, crickets
chirping (for that joke that falls short), a
trumpet, and more. You can also add fun
animated props like goggles and a snorkle,
strange hats, and facial hair (not necessary
for me).
Optimize Afterward
While on-air Hangouts create a YouTube video
automatically, that video is not optimized
automatically. Once the YouTube video of
your hangout is processed, you can go in and
optimize it. Add your keywords to the title of
the video, the tags, and the description. Also,
under advanced settings, you can set your
video location. This is important if you are
trying to rank for local keywords.
Skip the Webinar and Host
a Hangout Instead
Thinking of hosting a webinar? Why not
host a hangout instead? You can have
unlimited people watch. And, if you
encourage comments during the hangout,
all of the comments are saved under the
YouTube video and will greatly help in your
optimization efforts.
dcsLOCAL	www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 31 of 40 --

32 | June 2013
your webcam with your product on a Hangout and fire
away. Is your product digital? Share your screen and
walk someone through the details. Have someone else
on the Hangout to add dialogue or run the hangout on
your own. While a hangout can hold up to 10 people
with video sharing, you can also fly solo and create a
hangout video on your own.
3. Create a How-To Video
Again use screen sharing or get on camera and show
people how to do something related to your product or
category. Whiteboard videos are engaging and relatively
easy to create. One of the most common types of searches
on YouTube are “How to” searches. What questions are
people asking in your industry? Create a how-to Hangout
Video and watch the traffic come pouring in.
4. Do a Product or Company Review
Have a company or manufacturer you are trying to woo?
Why not do a positive review video of their product or
company and then post it to your blog and of course
YouTube. Then have the company you review link to it. It’s a
great way to build relationships and links back to your site.
Google Hangouts can also be a great tool for online
marketing consultants. Consider this angle. What if
you, as a consultant, offered to create and optimize a
video for a prospect for free, or for a nominal fee? How
impressed would they be, and how easy would it be
for you to execute that with Google Hangouts? Just set
up the video interview style and ask the prospect some
basic questions about their business, products, benefits,
etc. You could end up landing a very nice client while
providing a really valuable (and easy) service right up
front. Never has hanging out been more productive for
your business! DCS
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 32 of 40 --

Interviews With Alvern Bullard and Ronald Wiplinger by
Brittanie Byron, DCS Labs Editor
We decided to do something special this month and
share two DotComSecrets-X success stories. What’s
interesting is that both members had early success with
DCS-X, but the similarities end there.
The first is Alvern Bullard, a mother and IT sales rep living
in Ireland who immediately focused on solo advertising
to start growing her own list (something she says she’s
struggled with in the past). Alvern made her first sale on
day 21 of the DCS-X training and quickly built a great
start to her email list with 195 subscribers.
Ronald Wiplinger runs an internet marketing consulting
business in Taiwan. He made $523 within a few months
of joining DCS-X using traffic he was already generating
through his blogs and social media sources.
Together, Alvern and Ronald prove there’s more than
one way to get fast results. Here are their stories:
Q&A With Alvern Bullard
Q: Have you had any experience with internet
marketing in the past?
A: Yes, I have had both negative and positive
experiences. I have been involved in Internet marketing
for five years now. I have learned so much and I am
grateful for it. I am also the author of a book that is
available on iTunes called “Web Traffic 4 Idiots” and
dcsSUCCESS	www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 33 of 40 --

34 | May 2013
I have sold over 50 copies. I’ve made money through
Adsense and affiliate marketing as well.
Q: Why did you decide to sign up for DotComSecrets-X?
A: I know that the Internet marketing space is full of
innovation and I am open to learning new systems that
work that I can share with people I meet. I also know that
Russell offers great, quality products as I’ve purchased
from him in the past.
Q: What was your reaction when you made your first sale?
A: I was quite surprised as I know it can be difficult to make
a sale online.
Q: What is the biggest lesson you’ve learned about
internet marketing since joining DotComSecrets-X?
A: I never knew that solo ads existed so I suppose that
is the biggest aha moment for me because I have been
trying to build a list for years.
Alvern (above left) posted her first
success with DCS-X on Facebook.
Alvern’s first
DCS-X sale
using a paid
solo ad.
Daily marketing tips you can implement on your
commute to the office. Listen FREE by subscribing at:
www.MarketingInYourCar.com
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 34 of 40 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 35
Q: What is your goal over the next six months for growing
what you’ve already started?
A: My goal is to continue to grow my list to 20,000
subscribers and to fine tune my golden sequence to the
point that it is generating $1 per month per subscriber
for me.
Q: What’s on your vision board?
A: On my dream board are several goals which include:
• 	Building an Internet Marketing business that
generates $15,000 per month
• 	Retirement from my day job by age 41
• 	Spending more time with my two children
• 	Becoming an International Speaker on Internet Traffic
• 	Becoming a Philanthropist during my early
retirement years
• 	Purchasing my dream home
Q&A With Ronald Wiplinger
Q: What source of traffic have you been most successful
with so far?
A: I made some programs to use my 100 blogs, 25 Twitter
accounts, 20 Facebook accounts and other Social Media
outlets. I get about 11,000 clicks per day from all of these.
I do need to enhance my squeeze pages and SLOs. I also
use my banner service and AdSubmitters.
Q: What is the biggest lesson you’ve learned about
Internet marketing since joining DotComSecrets-X?
A: There is nothing more important than the list, how to
tickle people and keep them in at the same time. And the
winners are only the ones who sell the tools. It is like the
time when people washed for gold - only the suppliers of
tools got rich.
Q: What do you plan on doing with the extra money
you’ve earned so far?
A: Re-invest to increase my list to my goal of 50,000
members and hire some Virtual Assistants to get time for
my next big project.
Q: What is your goal over the next six months for growing
what you’ve already started?
A: I’m getting 3 to 10 new subscribers a day. With the new
programs in the works I believe I can increase that to 100
subscribers per day on average. That gives about 18,300
members over the next six months. DCS
Ronald’s first ClickBank
check for DCS-X earnings

-- 35 of 40 --

FREE SOLO ADS:
By Malcolm Robinson, MalcolmRobinsonsBlog.com
I have been sitting wondering how to start this article for
about a week now and I will tell you quite simply why...
I have a really hard time with the word FREE. You see I
am a little sceptical in that sense and don’t really believe
anything in life is free. So before we get down to the “Nuts
and Bolts” of the free traffic method of mailing to safe lists,
I want to get something of my chest. They are not FREE.
There I said it. They may not cost any money but I
promise they are not free. You see, these methods I am
about to talk about require a tremendous amount of
time in order for them to produce results and I like to call
it sweat money.
I am sure you will agree that your time is worth
something, right? Well this is the investment you need to
make in order for these methods to work for you.... The
more time (sweat money) you can invest the better your
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 36 of 40 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 37
results will be. And trust me, if done right they can be
amazing! Sound fair? Good.
Now that we have that cleared up, I’m sure we
can all agree you do need to invest something. It’s
quite important that you see it this way so that your
expectations of what is required of you are realistic.
What Is A Safelist Solo?
Before I get into some of the things you can do to
minimize your time investment and maximize your results
I just want to clear up what a safelist “solo” mailing is.
“A safelist is a form of e-mail marketing and advertising
where the members have agreed to receive each other’s
messages. Usually, the safelist administration reserves the
right to mail its' members. Types of safelists include paid,
credit based, and free.” (InternetMarketingDefinitions.com)
Step 1: Get A Dedicated Email Address
When signing up for safelists you are going to need to
give an e-mail address as a part of the process. Now
needless to say if you use your private e-mail address you
are going to be absolutely bombarded with mails and
no-one wants that. The best way to avoid this is to create
a dedicated email account for your safelists, and the best
place to do this is at a site called ViralInbox.com
It might take a little getting used to but it’s well worth the
effort. They have some great training on how to set up
dcs-X
14 POPULAR SAFELISTS
Viral URL
http://viralurl.com/
Listbuilding Maximizer
http://www.listbuildingmaximizer.com/
Business World List
http://www.businessworldlist.com/
Adchiever
http://www.adchiever.com/
List Bonus
http://listbonus.com/
Your EZ List
http://yourezlist.com/
Downline Builder Direct
http://www.downlinebuilderdirect.com/
Reactive Ads
http://reactiveads.info/
List Surfing
http://www.listsurfing.com/
Elite Safelist
http://www.elitesafelist.com/
State of the Art Mailer
(SOTAM)
http://state-of-the-art-mailer.com/
Prospect Geyser
http://www.prospectgeyser.com/
Advertize
http://www.advertyze.com/
Top Surfer
http://topsurfer.com/
Regardless of what method
you are using to drive traffic
the difference between those
who succeed and those who
fail is action, consistent action.”
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 37 of 40 --

38 | June 2013
and use their inbox system. I promise that learning to use
this tool will save you a lot of time and frustration.
Step 2: Get Organized
If you decide to use this method of driving traffic you can
get overwhelmed quickly and the best way to avoid this is
to get organized. My advice here is to set up an excel sheet
with all relevant information for each safelist site you will
be joining.
Here are a few important bits of information you need to
record:
• 	Site name and Login URL
• 	Username and password
• 	When, how often, and how much traffic can be sent
through the safelist.
• 	Track results for each safelist.
• 	It also helps to diarise the days when each list can be
mailed.
I cannot reiterate how important it is to remain consistent
when using free safelists. In the beginning it may feel like
you are not getting anywhere but I promise, it is those who
remain focused and consistent who win this game.
Step 3: Go Ahead And Sign Up
Once you have set up your viral inbox mail account, created
your excel sheet to keep things nice and organized, you are
ready to start signing up for some safelists. You can see a list
of the more popular ones on page 37.
Now please... don’t go and sign up for all of them all at once!
If there is another little piece of advice I can give it is to do it
slowly and systematically in order to acclimatize and not get
drowned by too much information.
So sign up for one or two sites. Get to know what you can
and cannot do and once you have the hang of them sign up
for two more. Remember that to win this game you need to
take baby steps.
What About The Paid Upgrades?
This is a question I often get because all of these sites that
you join will offer you some sort of paid upgrade or paid
membership option.
The first thing that needs to be said is that these upgrades
are not necessary in order for the free solo ads to work...
However, if chosen and done correctly they can dramatically
increase your results. You really need to evaluate each
upgrade on its own merits and make an educated decision
on whether you think its right for you. This often small
investment could end up in some serious results.
Copy, Paste, Send
Once you have everything set up you need to get your
email swipe ready, have your squeeze page link working,
make sure your auto responder follow up sequence is in
place... Then copy, paste and send. It’s as simple as that.
Procrastination is the assassination of success! Regardless
of what method you are using to drive traffic the difference
between those who succeed and those who fail is action,
consistent action. Do what you can do, as often, and
consistently as you can do it, and you will get there. And
remember to always have fun. DCS
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com
